description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, featuring vertical wooden staves held together by two prominent gray metal hoops—one near the top and one around the middle. The wood appears weathered, with a natural brown grain texture, and the metal bands provide structural reinforcement. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood as the sides, with the top rim slightly beveled. There are no visible handles, spouts, or openings, and the surface is matte with a rough, tactile finish. The barrel is devoid of any labels, markings, or decorative elements, giving it a utilitarian appearance. This type of barrel is commonly found in outdoor or warehouse settings for storage or decorative purposes.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with gray metal hoops and a rough, matte finish. It has a classic bulging shape and no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: The barrel's front shows the full curvature of the staves and the prominent metal hoops, with the wood grain and texture clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side view is nearly identical to the front, highlighting the symmetry and consistent banding of the barrel.
description_view_2: Another side view, showing the same features as the previous views, with no additional details or openings.
description_view_3: The rear view mirrors the front, with the same wood texture and metal reinforcement visible.
